content.problemStatement:
Consider an infinite sequence XiX_iXi‚Äã of nonnegative integers, in which X1=AX_1 = AX1‚Äã=A, X2=BX_2 = BX2‚Äã=B, X3=CX_3 = CX3‚Äã=C, and for i‚â•4i\ge 4i‚â•4,
Xi=max‚Å°(Xi‚àí3,Xi‚àí2,Xi‚àí1)‚àímin‚Å°(Xi‚àí3,Xi‚àí2,Xi‚àí1)X_i = \max(X_{i-3}, X_{i-2}, X_{i-1}) - \min(X_{i-3}, X_{i-2}, X_{i-1})Xi‚Äã=max(Xi‚àí3‚Äã,Xi‚àí2‚Äã,Xi‚àí1‚Äã)‚àímin(Xi‚àí3‚Äã,Xi‚àí2‚Äã,Xi‚àí1‚Äã)

Find the KKK-th term of this sequence.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers A,B,C,KA, B, C, KA,B,C,K ‚Äî the first three  terms of the sequence, and the index of the term that you have to find.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the KKK-th term of the corresponding sequence.
